defaultRoutes.purpose: Establish authority within the first scroll: a confident hero, the firm's expertise areas in a structured grid, named partners or trust markers (clients, awards, accreditations) and a measured CTA towards a discovery conversation.

Spell out the practice areas the firm serves — corporate law, M&A, tax, dispute resolution, audit, advisory — in groups so a prospect can self-qualify. Replaces a flat 'services' list with a structured taxonomy a buying-side counsel actually reads.
Tell the firm's story — founding partners, professional philosophy, ethical posture, history — so a serious client recognises a known counterparty rather than a faceless brand.
Per-office address, direct lines and a formal enquiry form. Treats contact as a respect signal: the firm is reachable, in named offices, with named people.

optionalRoutes.when: the firm leads with named partners and counsels whose individual credentials, bar admissions or specialisations carry conversion weight
the firm differentiates by served industries (banking, life sciences, energy, real estate, technology) and wants prospects to recognise relevant precedent
the firm publishes commentary, white papers or case notes as part of its business-development engine
